scenario_name: Self-hosted open-weights model deployment in private cloud
description: An enterprise self-hosts an open-weights LLM in a private-cloud enclave to avoid sending content to third-party providers. The deployment uses a popular inference server, an internal model registry, and serves multiple internal applications via an internal API gateway. Open-weights deployments inherit a different risk profile: extraction is trivial for any insider, alignment is brittle (refusal-direction abliteration is cheap), and supply-chain integrity for downloaded weights becomes a first-order concern.
assumptions: Self-hosted in an enterprise-managed private cloud.
Weights are pulled from a public registry on initial deployment; re-pulls require approval.
Inference server runs in a container with a tightly scoped service account.

components.description: Open-source inference server (vLLM-class) running on GPU nodes.
Internal model registry holding the pinned weight version with hash attestation.
Input + output safety classifiers; output secret-scanner.
Corporate IDP issuing per-app tokens for the internal API gateway.
Per-request audit log; GPU-utilization and cost dashboards.

risks_identified.rationale: Initial weight pull is a supply-chain trust event; subsequent edits inside the org are a separate trust event.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Hash attestation on pinned weight; internal change-control on weight updates.

risks_identified.rationale: Open-weights deployment makes insider model exfiltration trivial; weights are crown-jewel IP for any fine-tuned adapter on top.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Hardware-backed key custody; egress controls; access auditing.

risks_identified.rationale: White-box access enables membership inference and reconstruction against any fine-tune.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Differential-privacy training planned but not yet deployed.

risks_identified.rationale: Open-weights models can be cheaply abliterated; downstream internal teams may run an abliterated variant.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Internal registry is the only sanctioned source; allowlist enforcement at the gateway.

risks_identified.rationale: All internal AI workloads depend on the same inference cluster; outage propagates.
Internal API gateway is shared across many internal applications; one misbehaving caller can affect others.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Per-app token, per-app rate-limit, per-app log namespace.

risks_identified.rationale: Per-request audit logs contain prompts from many internal apps; broad exposure if access not controlled.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Logs partitioned per app; access role-gated.

existing_controls.description: Hash attestation on pinned weight; internal change-control gates weight updates.
Hardware-backed key custody on weight storage; egress audited.
Internal registry is the only sanctioned model source; allowlist enforced at gateway.
Per-app tokens, per-app rate-limits, per-app log namespace.
Input + output safety classifiers; output secret-scanner.

control_gaps.description: Differential-privacy training not yet deployed for fine-tunes on top of the base.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Adopt DP-SGD for adapter training; track epsilon per release.

control_gaps.description: No automated detection of abliterated-variant deployments inside the org.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Run periodic refusal-stability evaluation against deployed model versions; alert on deviation from the attested baseline.

control_gaps.description: Cross-region failover is planned but not implemented.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Stand up a warm-standby cluster; exercise failover quarterly.
